repairs_needed: REPAIR 1 (word space, mandatory): extend the connector layer beyond length 4 at least for the eight corner-type menu steps whose 2-4-step spaces are tiny (|D|=46 for (±2,±2,∓2)-class, 394-600 for near-corner). Measured: |D_5| = 47,421 for step (2,2,-2) (vs 46), and the extended space D_{2-5} has live mass 0.107 / 0.194 / 0.066 at the three jammed segments — the counterexamples disappear with real margin. All L3 constants must be re-measured on the extended space (it also shrinks per-point kill granularity from 1/46 to ~1/47k on the fragile steps).

REPAIR 2 (honest ledger accounting): the stated constants confuse position-mass/expectation with word-union-mass. Exact word-mass numbers on the certified walk: own-line fatal at birth (anchors-only) is mean 0.190, p99 0.522, MAX 0.717 (not a uniform 0.317); realized received cross damage is mean 0.366, p90 0.546, MAX 1.000 (not 0.10). The total realized BAD mass at stitch time: mean 0.556, p99 0.913, max 1.0; adding the 1/3 Markov tax the stated union exceeds 1 at 674/2457 = 27.4% of real segments. Any repaired ledger must close against these numbers, pointwise.

REPAIR 3 (replace the plain 3x-average future-tax with per-receiver protection): the Markov tax bounds each donor's TOTAL inflicted damage but nothing prevents concentration on one receiver; demonstrated constructively (receiver 1465: 21 sequentially-legal earlier donor words drive its bad mass 0.144 -> 0.754; receiver 26: 7 donors, 0.00 -> 0.805; 9 of 12 sampled adversarial words SATISFY the 3x tax, ratios 0.63-2.46). Fold into the construction per-(donor,receiver) damage caps c_{i,j} decaying with spatial 3-shell — justified by measured per-donor damage decay (median a-priori E-damage 0.188 / 0.027 / 0.0009 / ~0 for shells [3,9),[9,27),[27,81),[81+); max single-legal-word damage 0.65 / 0.59 / 0.23 / 0.15) and by time-locality of realized received damage (60.4% from donors <=3 segments back, 81.4% <=9, 91.8% <=27). Cap-violation mass at stitch i is then bounded by Markov+union: sum_j e(i,j)/c_{i,j}, and the receiver invariant received_j <= sum_i c_{i,j} = beta replaces the false 0.10 constant.

REPAIR 4 (stitch order): stitch fragile segments (ascending |D(s_i)|) first, so small-word-space segments choose while the level is near anchors-only (their bad mass <= anchors-only max 0.717 on |D|=46 steps; <= 0.52 p99 overall) and large-|D| segments absorb received damage where kill granularity is diluted. The left-to-right greedy order is not load-bearing anywhere; segment stitching only needs anchors + previously-stitched segments as obstacles.

REPAIR 5 (restate the lemma relative to the invariant): L3 must be stated as 'every state reachable BY THE REPAIRED CONSTRUCTION (which enforces the caps) admits a stitch word', not 'any walk the construction can reach' — the unqualified form is false even without adversaries (the certified walk itself reached three states with empty 2-4-step word spaces).
attacks.proof_sketch_or_counterexample: COUNTEREXAMPLE (exact, machine-verified, on the certified instance itself — no adversary needed). Probability space, stated cleanly: for menu step s in S (the 124 nonzero vectors of [-2,2]^3) let D(s) = all words w of length 2-4 over S with step-sum M.s whose own 3-5 points (including both endpoints) are distinct and triple-free; Omega_i = D(s_i) with the UNIFORM measure on words (positions then carry the collar multiplicity weights — 'words weighted by multiplicity'); |D(s)| ranges 46 (corner steps) to 305,966. Completeness partition: with all anchors pre-placed and segments stitched in order, every collinear triple of the finished level is excluded by exactly one of (i) three anchors — L2 inheritance, verified exactly: cross(Mu,Mv) = cof(M).cross(u,v), cof(M) = ((9,0,0),(0,-3,-9),(0,9,0)), det 729; (ii) word-internal; (iii) E1: one interior of the current segment + two existing points; (iv) E2: two interiors + one existing point. Hence BAD_i = E1 union E2 at stitch time is the exhaustive per-segment constraint and families (a),(b),(d) of the ledger are just a decomposition of P_i(BAD_i); (c) is not a safety constraint but a control mechanism. RESULTS on all 2457 final-level segments of amplified-193-8292 (exact integer arithmetic): P_i(BAD_i) mean 0.556, median 0.561, p90 0.760, p99 0.913, MAX 1.0000. Segments 546, 1074 (step (2,2,-2)) and 1429 (step (-2,-2,2)) have ZERO legal 2-4-step words at their realized greedy stitch time — independently re-verified with the project's own legal_against predicate — and these are exactly the three segments where the walk generator (which permits length <=12) was forced to emit length-5 stitches: the certified walk exists only because the implementation exceeded the lemma's word space. Pointwise union with the 1/3 Markov tax term: >= 1 at 674/2457 segments (27.4%), max 1.3333. Answer to hostile Q1: worst realized received damage = 1.0000 (segment 546: anchors-only bad mass 0.000, all 46 words killed by earlier segments' interiors); mean realized received = 0.366 — 3.7x the ledger's 0.10, and the 0.317 'own-line' constant is likewise an artifact of measuring position-mass/expectation instead of word-union-mass (exact anchors-only word mass: mean 0.190, max 0.717). Answer to hostile Q2: the union bound was checked pointwise on ALL 2457 segments, not 200; it fails at 27% of them. The averaging step of the Markov argument is additionally unsound for adversarial reachable states: a single LEGAL donor word kills up to 0.65 of a receiver's word space, and greedy adversarial-but-legal choices at 7-21 earlier donors (most satisfying the 3x tax) drive receivers' bad mass to 0.75-0.81 (lower bounds; cross-kills between adversarial points not even counted). WHAT SURVIVES: the machinery validates (60/60 realized stitch words are non-BAD at their own time; the 3 jams match the 3 length-5 stitches exactly), damage is strongly local in space (per-donor E-damage decays ~7-30x per 3-shell) and time (60% of received mass from <=3 segments back), and the extended D_{2-5} space un-jams all counterexamples with 0.07-0.19 live mass — so a repaired lemma (length-5 layer on fragile steps + per-receiver caps + fragile-first order + honest word-mass constants) is plausibly provable, but it is a different, stronger-construction lemma, and its margins at the worst segments (~0.07) are thin enough that L4 scale-invariance must be re-established for the repaired constants.
attacks.computations_run: All exact integer arithmetic, PyPy, scripts in /tmp, data from /Users/erik/homelab/math193. (1) /tmp/l3_recon.py — rebuilt the final level of amplified-193-8292 from provenance-8292.json: 2458 anchors, 2457 segments, all displacements verified = M.s with s in the 124-menu; realized stitch length histogram {2:53, 3:1433, 4:968, 5:3}; connector layer stats (7,114,584 words; per-step |D| 46-305,966; 22-520 distinct interior offsets/step). (2) /tmp/l3_sweep.py (4 workers, ~80 CPU-min) — exact per-segment BAD word-mass for ALL 2457 segments under greedy semantics with event-activation tags: repeated-point, E1 (interior + 2 existing) and E2 (2 interiors + 1 existing) events, giving m_anchor (anchors-only), m_greedy (realized stitch time), m_full, received = m_greedy - m_anchor, and a donor-distance histogram of received mass; merged in /tmp/l3_merged.json via /tmp/l3_analyze.py. (3) /tmp/l3_validate.py — 60/60 randomly sampled realized stitch words verified non-BAD at their own stitch time (end-to-end pipeline validation); plus exact match of the 3 empty-word-space segments {546,1074,1429} with the 3 realized length-5 stitches. (4) Independent re-verification of the 3 counterexamples using amplify193.legal_against (project code, not mine): 0 live words each. (5) /tmp/l3_adversary.py + /tmp/l3_adversary2.py — for 8+ receivers: per-poison-point kill masses over all donor collar positions within R=130 (+far controls), per-donor a-priori expected damage (word-occupancy weighted), and exact max damage over LEGAL donor words; shell-binned decay table from 514 (receiver,donor) pairs. (6) /tmp/l3_democounter.py — constructive adversary: receiver 26 bad mass 0.00->0.805 with 7 sequentially-legal earlier donors; receiver 1465 0.144->0.754 with 21; restricted 3x-tax check on chosen words (9 of 12 tax-compliant). (7) /tmp/l3_repair5.py — meet-in-the-middle enumeration of the full length-5 connector layer for the corner steps (|D_5| = 47,421) and exact extended-space BAD mass at the three jammed segments (live mass 0.107/0.194/0.066). (8) Exact verification of the L2 cofactor identity cross(Mu,Mv)=cof(M)cross(u,v) used by the completeness partition.
attacks.decisive_next_computation: Simulate the REPAIRED construction end-to-end on the certified level-4 walk and re-run this exact pointwise audit on it. Concretely: (a) enumerate length-5 connector layers for every step with |D_2-4| < ~2000 (meet-in-the-middle, minutes each); (b) stitch level 5 in ascending-|D| order, enforcing per-receiver caps c_{i,j} = min(3*e_bar(shell(i,j)), cap) with shell constants taken from the measured decay table (0.188/0.027/0.0009 per 3-shell) and receiver budget beta ~ 0.25; (c) at every stitch record the exact forbidden mass P_i(BAD_i) + sum_{j unstitched} e(i,j)/c_{i,j}; the repaired L3 is viable iff the max over all segments stays below 1 with visible margin (target <= 0.85). Then repeat the identical audit one level deeper (level 6, ~7400 segments) to test whether the repaired constants are level-invariant — that single pair of numbers (max forbidden mass at level 5 vs level 6) is what L4 must make into a theorem, and it decides whether the whole chain closes.

repairs_needed: Restate L4(i) as GEOMETRIC CONVERGENCE to a limit distribution, not exact level-invariance: statistics at radius d converge only once level k >~ log_3(d)+2; measured transient contraction of successive differences is x0.074/level at d=27 and x0.222/level at d=81. The mechanism must be in the statement: remote-branch (fold) contamination at fixed scale d decays geometrically (P(fold<=27): 17.3% -> 5.7% -> 1.1% -> 0.0% across levels 3-6) because M triples remote distances while stitching adds <=8.

L4(ii) far-field: state as a uniform per-shell decay bound (measured ratio 0.34-0.61 per 3x shell, all levels/ensembles where untruncated) PLUS one-new-shell-per-level accumulation: each level appends one outer shell whose mass is ~0.42-0.46x the previous, so per-level ledger constants INCREASE monotonically toward a finite geometric limit - the lemma must bound this convergent creep, not assert constancy. The decay mechanism (lattice-incidence thinning ~1/r beating pair growth r^{2D}, D=log lambda/log 3~1.11) needs an actual proof.

Fix the L3/L4 metric conflation: the ledger's 0.317 'own-line fatal mass' is a PER-POSITION multiplicity-weighted collar mass; exact PER-WORD fatality is ~2.76x larger (union nearly additive: measured completed-level word mass 0.688+-0.011 at L3, at-birth word mass 0.49-0.53). The '0.317+0.10+0.333+0.028=0.78<1' arithmetic is invalid per-word as written. Repair: restate L3 as worst-case availability >=1 word (measured min: 4 available words at birth, 1 word vs completed level) with L4 claiming level-invariance of the availability DISTRIBUTION, or rebuild the budget per-position with explicit union accounting.

The 2-4-step word model must include the bottleneck subpopulation and its escape hatch: steps with the minimum 46-word domain are 11.2-14.1% of segments at every level (no drift); ALL 12 length-5 stitches in the certified walk (3 at L5, 9 at L6; jam rate ~1% of 46-word segments, level-stable) occur exactly at these letters, i.e. the realized construction already exits the 2-4 model ~0.11% of the time.

The residual monotone creep in at-birth word fatality (+3.5+-3.2%/level boosted L3->L4; +4.1+-2.7%/level over levels 3-6 small-sample) is unresolved at current precision: it must be proven to contract (the shell-appending model predicts increments shrinking x~0.45/level toward a limit ~0.52-0.55) or bounded via the containment at-birth <= completed-level (at-birth obstacle set is a subset of the completed level's point set). Decisive test = level 7.

Edge/interior distinction: at every level a boundary population exists (early-walk segments are systematically hotter: L3 completed position mass 0.283 in the first walk quintile vs 0.248 later; d=81 statistics at level 3 have essentially no interior sample). Lemma statements must be about interior segments with the boundary fraction shown to be O(1) segments, not level-growing.
attacks.proof_sketch_or_counterexample:
SETUP (all exact-integer, on the certified seed-193 run; levels 0-6 = 21/63/215/716/2458/8293/28272 points reconstructed exactly: provenance parents give levels 0-5, and all 8293 level-6 anchors were matched IN ORDER as M-images of level-5 points, ending at the final point — the recursion 'level k+1 = M(level k) anchors + stitches' is machine-verified, with stitch excursion from start anchor <=8 (mean 3.31-3.36, level-flat; hard bound 24)).

SKETCH OF THE REPAIRED L4. (i) Local fixed point: define the local configuration law mu_k(d) = distribution over interior segments of the point configuration within Chebyshev distance d. The renormalization map R (apply M, refill each anchor gap with its stitch word) satisfies: anchors inside the M-image of a parent d-ball are EXACTLY the parent configuration (verified identity), and refill is uniformly bounded (excursion <=8). Empirically R is a contraction: mean counts N_d converge with successive differences shrinking x0.074 (d=27) and x0.222 (d=81) per level; two-sample KS between consecutive levels falls below the 95% critical value for all d<=27 by L5->L6 (D=0.023 vs crit 0.031); the refill factor rho_d is level-stable (pooled 3.33-3.37 = 0.985-0.988 x global growth lambda) and SELF-CORRECTING (corr(parent-density, rho) = -0.32 to -0.42 at every level; dense neighborhoods refill at 3.20-3.25, sparse at 3.39-3.47 — mean-reversion, i.e. the contraction property). The only level-dependent term is fold contamination, which recedes geometrically (P(fold<=27): 17.3/5.7/1.1/0.0%; P(<=81): 97.7/29.0/11.7/4.1%, x~0.35-0.40 per level) because folds are magnified x3 while stitches fatten branches by <=16. Hence mu_k(d) -> mu_inf(d) geometrically; extrapolated limits: N3=3.99, N9=11.2, N27=34.61, N81=112.6+-0.7. (ii) Far-field: fatal mass is local and its shell decomposition decays geometrically at a LEVEL-INVARIANT rate where shells are untruncated: completed-level L3 shares 0.8/24.6/41.8/23.6/8.7/0.4% with ratios 0.566[0.541,0.592] and 0.369[0.345,0.394] (bootstrap 68% CI); at-birth L3 ratios 0.520/0.344, L4 0.609/0.457/0.424 — ratio ~<0.5 per 3x shell, consistent with dimension counting r^{2D-3}, D=1.113 (=0.42/shell). Summability follows IF this ratio is proved <1 uniformly; totals then converge geometrically as each level appends one outer shell. (iii) Induction: per-level existence needs worst-case availability >0, not average: measured min available words at birth = 4/46 (same bottleneck letter at L3 and L4), plus the length-5 escape (used 12x, jam rate level-stable ~1%).

MEASURED DRIFT TABLE (levels 3->6; WLS slope with SE; 'contraction' = ratio of successive increments):
- N(d=3) interior: 4.06(5)/3.99(3)/4.01(1)/3.99(1) -> -0.37+-0.25 %/level, oscillating: CONVERGED.
- N(d=9): 11.70(18)/11.19(5)/11.18(3)/11.21(1) -> -0.00+-0.17 %/level: CONVERGED from L4.
- N(d=27): 45.02(189)/36.08(30)/34.72(9)/34.62(5) -> linear -0.99+-0.25 %/level BUT increments -8.94/-1.36/-0.10 contract x0.074: CONVERGENT TRANSIENT, remaining change ~ -0.01.
- N(d=81), L4-6: 142.9(29)/119.4(6)/114.1(2) -> -5.78+-0.50 %/level, contraction x0.222: CONVERGENT TRANSIENT, limit 112.6+-0.7.
- refill rho(d=27): 3.381(6)/3.327(3)/3.368(1) -> +0.45+-0.07 %/level, OSCILLATING in lockstep with lambda (rho/lambda = 0.985/0.986/0.988): no drift.
- mean stitch length (=lambda): 3.341(34)/3.436(20)/3.375(11)/3.409(6) -> +0.33+-0.21 %/level, oscillating.
- fold P(<=27): 17.3/5.7/1.1/0.0% -> geometric decay x~0.3/level: IMPROVING with level.
- 46-word bottleneck letter share: 11.2/14.1/11.8/12.6%: FLAT. Step-mix mean log10(domain): 3.589(59)/3.469(34)/3.584(18)/3.539(10): oscillating.
- length-5 jam rate: 0/0/0.12/0.11%: flat after onset.
- completed-level position fatal mass: L3 0.2564+-0.0047 (full 210 segs); L4/L5/L6 partial running means 0.265/0.272/0.280 (early-walk-biased upward by ~+0.01-0.02; finals pending in /tmp/l4_fatal2_L*_s*.pkl) -> apparent +~3 %/level creep, consistent with the one-new-shell-per-level model; UNRESOLVED whether increments contract — flagged as the open drift.
- completed-level word fatal mass: L3 0.688+-0.011 (max 0.978, min availability 1 word); L4-L6 pending.
- AT-BIRTH word fatal mass (the construction-relevant ensemble): boosted L3 0.4928+-0.0122 (n=210), L4 0.5104+-0.0103 (n=300) -> +3.5+-3.2 %/level; small-sample L5 0.5098+-0.0281, L6 0.5334+-0.0364; 4-level WLS +4.1+-2.7 %/level, MONOTONE — the sharpest threat. Mitigations: (a) structurally capped by completed-level mass (at-birth obstacles are a subset of the completed level); (b) at-birth non-local share grows exactly as the appended-shell model predicts (F(>81)/F: 7.7/9.3/13.1%), with per-shell decay 0.42-0.46 -> predicted convergence to ~0.52-0.55 << 1. NOT a counterexample at current precision, but the single number a hostile referee will attack.

VERDICT ON THE THREE HOSTILE QUESTIONS: (1) The recursion is exactly true (machine-verified) and the local law converges geometrically to a fixed point — demonstrated at d=3..81 across levels 3-6, with the transient mechanism (fold recession) identified and quantified. (2) The shell decay RATE is level-invariant within bootstrap error where shells are untruncated (0.34-0.61 per shell; no trend), but the SUPPORT grows one shell per level, producing a convergent (geometric) creep in totals — the lemma must absorb this. (3) Yes, monotone drifts exist: N27/N81 (benign, contracting x0.07-0.22, bounded limits) and at-birth/completed fatal mass (+3-4 %/level, contraction unresolved, bounded by ceiling arguments; decisive at level 7). No measured statistic drifts in the dangerous direction without an identified convergent mechanism.
attacks.computations_run: All exact integer arithmetic, PyPy 3.11 on Eriks-MBP; scripts + artifacts in /tmp, data from /Users/erik/homelab/math193. (1) /tmp/l4_recon.py: exact reconstruction of levels 0-6 with segment structure from amplified-193-8292.txt + amplified-193-28271.txt + provenance-8292.json; verified anchors(level k+1) == M*(points level k) for ALL levels (8293/8293 matched in order at level 6); gap histograms (stitch lengths 2-5) -> /tmp/l4_levels.pkl. (2) /tmp/l4_local.py: counts of level-L points within Chebyshev d=3/9/27/81 of all segment midpoints, all/interior, quantiles + two-sample KS between consecutive levels; refill ratios rho_d via exact M-image pullback balls (|w0|<=3d, |w1|<=3d, |3w2-w1|<=9d) -> /tmp/l4_local_results.pkl. (3) /tmp/l4_rho_cond.py: corr(parent-density, rho) and rho by parent-density tercile, levels 3->4,4->5,5->6. (4) fold-distance distribution (min Chebyshev distance to walk-remote points, index gap>100) per level; fold-contamination fractions. (5) stitch-excursion bounds; 46-word bottleneck-letter frequency and length-5 jam census; step-mix domain-size drift. (6) /tmp/l4_fatal2b.py (sharded, 8 shards): per-segment fatal thresholds via pair-completion distance (direction-hash collinearity, obstacles sorted by distance) for all 2102 collar offsets x completed-level obstacle sets; multiplicity-weighted position mass + exact per-word mass at cutoffs 3..6561,inf; L3 complete (210 segs: posmass 0.2564+-0.0047, shells, bootstrap decay CIs), L4 (300) L5 (100) L6 (42) shards were ~50-60% complete at report time (partial means quoted; finals land in /tmp/l4_fatal2_L{4,5,6}_s*.pkl, analyzed by /tmp/l4_drift.py). (7) /tmp/l4_fatal3b.py: AT-BIRTH per-word fatality (obstacles = placed points + future anchors, the stitcher's exact legality set) with position shells: L3 n=210, L4 n=300 complete; L5 n=40, L6 n=24 small samples; boosted L5 (200) and L6 (150) auto-launched. (8) Calibration: brute-force cross-product verification of the collinearity/fatality logic on real segments; reproduction of the programme's reference metric (fatal positions ~460/seg of 2102, mass ~0.26-0.28 vs logged 0.317 incl. own-stitch/contamination differences); verification that actually-chosen stitch words are never fatal at birth.
attacks.decisive_next_computation: Build level 7 of the same certified run (~96,400 steps; rerun the amplify_rich.py pipeline with seed 193, identical parameters, cap raised to ~100000 — RNG consumption reproduces levels 0-6 exactly, then constructs level 7; estimated hours-to-overnight in PyPy on this machine), then rerun this battery (/tmp/l4_local.py, /tmp/l4_fatal2b.py, /tmp/l4_fatal3b.py) on levels 4-7. It adjudicates every open point with pre-registered predictions: N81(L7) = 112.6+-0.7 and KS(L6,L7) at d=81 below critical (fixed point) vs N81 continuing to fall linearly (drift); at-birth word fatality 0.52-0.54 (convergent shell-appending model) vs >=0.55 (linear creep — L4 in danger); completed-level position-mass increment L6->L7 <= +0.004 with visible contraction vs another +0.008 (unbounded creep); shell decay ratios 0.42-0.57 unchanged including the newly populated (2187,6561] shell; P(fold<=81) ~ 1.4%; bottleneck jam rate ~1% flat. Secondary: finish the in-flight L4/L5/L6 completed-level shards (/tmp/l4_fatal2_L*_s*.pkl) and boosted at-birth L5/L6, then run /tmp/l4_drift.py and /tmp/l4_ab_analysis.py for the final four-level drift table at +-0.5%/level precision.

repairs_needed: Restate the L3 own-line term in the correct units: the published 0.317 is a POSITION mass (multiplicity-weighted share of the 2102-offset collar; total multiplicity 21,286,132 / 7,114,584 words = 2.99 positions/word). The quantity the counting argument needs is the excluded WORD fraction, which measures 0.48-0.55 mean / 0.85 p90 / 0.91-0.96 max at decision time on every instance including the certified seed-193 walk. The budget 0.317+0.10+0.333+0.028=0.78<1 becomes ~0.96 in the mean and up to ~1.42 pointwise; the '22% headroom' does not exist.

Weaken the quantifier: 'any walk the construction can reach' is refuted by an explicit reachable configuration (probe777). L3 must quantify over walks reachable by the SPECIFIC randomized router together with an inductively maintained invariant (e.g., a local-density/clearance cap ~ the adversarial run reached 11-13x the random-run level-1 density before jamming) that excludes the jam states, plus a proof that the router preserves the invariant.

Make the existence argument per-step: connector domain sizes range 46 to 305,966 words; a 46-word step (corner vectors, e.g. menu index 4) was starved to 2 surviving words on a plain random run (seed 42, L3, seg 81). Either the router must avoid small-domain letters at the previous level or availability must be proven for the worst step, not on average.

Since hard segments have only a handful of survivors (2/46 random; 0/281,312 adversarial), the remaining ledger families (cross damage 0.10, future-tax 1/3, triple-newborn 0.028) can no longer be subtracted as mass; pointwise simultaneity (skeleton gap #2) must be proven for the specific surviving words.

L4 should be restated for the corrected word-level constant. Good news: the exact birth word-kill mean is itself level-stable (0.48-0.55 at L3/L4/L5) and instance-stable across 7 seeds, so a repaired scale-invariance lemma is plausible - but it protects a budget that no longer closes by counting alone.
attacks.proof_sketch_or_counterexample: COUNTEREXAMPLE (machine-verified, exact). Construction state: seed-777 run of the identical construction schema (124-step radius-2 menu, M=((3,0,0),(0,0,-3),(0,3,-1)), anchors = M-images, global legality with future anchors as obstacles, stitch cap 16 = the wiggle route's own cap), with a crowd-hugging (density-maximizing) but fully legal stitch policy. Level 1 completed and CERTIFIED triple-free (142 steps, density 0.0252 vs 0.0022 for the random policy). During level 2, at segment 30/142 (bridging anchors 30->31, step s=(-1,0,0), gap M.s=(-3,0,0), 215 points legally placed): own-line fatal position-mass = 0.9796 (1972/2102 collar offsets), and EVERY one of the 281,312 legal 2-4-step connector words for this gap is killed by own-line constraints alone (SAFE=0, exhaustive enumeration from connector_domains4.pkl); randomized iterative deepening to depth 16 with 300k nodes x 20 tries found no stitch of any length, and 6/6 full restarts of the level jammed. Hence 'given any walk the construction can reach, every segment admits a stitch word' (L3) is false as stated. SECOND, INDEPENDENT DEFECT: the published constant 0.317 reproduces (my replication: 0.32-0.34 across seeds/levels) only as a collar POSITION mass; the exact excluded WORD fraction at decision time - the quantity the 'safe word exists by counting' step requires - is 0.48-0.55 mean, 0.91-0.96 per-segment max on random instances, and the worst measured random segment retained just 2 of 46 words before the other three ledger terms are even applied. CROSS-SEED TABLE (own-line fatal mass, published collar methodology, mean [per-segment max]): L3 - 193:0.3147(published), 7:0.3514[0.5812], 42:0.3206[0.5159], 101:0.3417[0.4438], 555:0.3437[0.4852], 2024:0.3154[0.5071], 31337:0.3161[0.4718]. L4 - 193:0.3236[0.5070](exact provenance; published 0.3388), 7:0.3345[0.6174], 42:0.3288[0.4876], 101:0.3308[0.4206], 555:0.3425[0.4983], 2024:0.3253[0.4647], 31337:0.3353[0.5241]. L5 - 193:0.3378[0.4546](24 segs; published 0.3170/0.3149). EXACT WORD-KILL at birth (mean/max): 193 L4 0.538/0.913, L5 0.477/0.908; 7 L3 0.549/0.871, L4 0.516/0.854; 42 L3 0.482/0.957, L4 0.507/0.786; 101 L3 0.479/0.913, L4 0.525/0.826; 555 L3 0.491/0.935, L4 0.521/0.919; 2024 L3 0.509/0.804, L4 0.528/0.799. Conclusion: the MEAN position-mass constant is genuinely instance/level-stable (0.315-0.351, never above 0.36 - the 0.45 alarm is NOT triggered in the mean, so L4's invariance claim survives), but per-segment maxima exceed 0.45 on 6/7 seeds (up to 0.62), the constant is in the wrong units for L3, and the L3 quantifier admits an exact zero-availability counterexample. The chain is salvageable only via a router-invariant-restricted quantifier and a per-step absolute-count existence argument.
attacks.computations_run: (1) Methodology calibration on the certified walk (amplified-193-8292.txt + provenance-8292.json): reproduced logs/fatal-mass.log's 490.8 fatal positions / 0.3170 as the multiplicity-weighted collar fraction with obstacles = the ENTIRE completed level (my 24-seg estimate: 516.7/0.3378; birth-time obstacles give only 0.187), plus exact level-4 reconstruction via M-preimages (2458 pts, 716 anchors verified). Scripts /tmp/calibrate193{,b,c}.py, /tmp/calib_exact_193.py; logs /tmp/calib193-L4.log, /tmp/calib-exact.log. (2) Six independent construction runs (seeds 7,42,101,555,2024,31337; identical machinery to amplify_rich/provenance193, deterministic replay verified by matching level lengths across reruns), levels 0-4 (~2100-2460 pts), all levels verified triple-free; own-line fatal mass measured at L3+L4 for each seed under both obstacle variants, 60-120 segments/level: /tmp/cross_seed_run{,2}.py -> /tmp/cross2-{7,42,101,555,2024}.json, /tmp/cross-31337.json. (3) Exact word-level kill fractions for every measured segment from connector_domains4.pkl (7.11M words; per-step domains 46-305,966), giving the corrected ledger constant and worst-segment survivor counts. (4) Two adversarial-but-legal density-maximizing runs (/tmp/adversarial_run.py, seeds 666 slack-2 and 777 slack-4): level-1 walks verified triple-free at 11-13.5x normal density; 777 jammed level 2 on 6/6 restarts, 666 failed to finish level 2 in 2.5h. (5) Jam probe (/tmp/probe777.py, /tmp/probe777b.py; state in /tmp/probe777_state.pkl): deterministic replay to the first jam, exhaustive enumeration of all 281,312 candidate 2-4-step words (0 safe, all own-line-killed, position-mass 0.9796), and a 6M-node randomized search to depth 16 finding no stitch of any length.
attacks.decisive_next_computation: A complete, non-budgeted existence check at the jammed configuration (/tmp/probe777_state.pkl): exhaustive BFS/DFS over ALL legal stitch paths from anchor 30 to anchor 31 up to length ~8 (the legal neighbourhood is tiny - the 300k-node searches exhausted instantly - so full enumeration is feasible), to certify 'zero stitches of ANY length' rather than 'zero 2-4-step words'. If certified, L3's quantifier is dead beyond repair-by-longer-words and the invariant route becomes mandatory. Immediately after: compute the max-local-density (points per radius-9 ball) profile for the 7 random runs vs the 2 adversarial runs at each level, propose the separating cap as the router invariant, and re-run the jam search restricted to invariant-respecting configurations - if no jam is found there and the exact word-kill stays bounded below 1 per step (including the 46-word domains), the repaired L3 has a concrete provable form.

repairs_needed: R1 (word space, mandatory): extend the connector layer to length 5 for every step with |D_2-4| < ~2000, in particular the eight corner-class steps with |D|=46 (|D_5| ~ 47k there). Non-negotiable: the certified walk amplified-193-8292 itself contains exactly 3 length-5 stitches (my independent reconstruction: corner steps (2,2,-2), (2,2,-2), (-2,-2,2)), and at all three the full 2-4-step space has ZERO legal words at realized stitch time (verified exactly, including E2 events). L3 as written is false on its own certified instance.

R2 (units, mandatory): every ledger constant must be restated as exact word-union mass, not multiplicity-weighted collar position mass. Independently confirmed: on 24 sampled segments at decision time, position mass mean 0.246 / max 0.362 vs E1-only word-kill mean 0.565 / max 0.734 (ratio 2.0-2.6x, and true kill is higher since E2 is excluded). The published 0.317+0.10+0.333+0.028=0.78<1 budget does not close; in honest units the pointwise ledger exceeds 1 at ~27% of segments (attack 1) and my E1-only mean+Markov tax alone gives 0.90 mean / 1.07 max.

R3 (quantifier): restate L3 over states reachable by the REPAIRED construction carrying an explicit inductive invariant, not 'any walk the construction can reach' — that form is refuted twice: by the certified walk's own three jams and by the adversarial-but-legal probe777 state (zero stitches of any length <= 16 found, own-line position mass 0.98). The invariant (per-receiver caps + effective density bound) becomes part of L5's induction hypothesis.

R4 (cross-damage accounting): replace the flat 3x-average Markov future-tax and the false 0.10 received-cross constant with per-(donor,receiver) caps c_{i,j} decaying with spatial 3-shell (measured donor E-damage decay 0.188/0.027/0.0009/~0 per shell); receiver invariant sum_i c_{i,j} = beta (~0.25) replaces 0.10 (realized received damage is actually mean 0.366, max 1.0 — one receiver was fully killed by earlier donors). Cap-violation mass bounded by Markov+union per stitch.

R5 (stitch order): stitch fragile segments (ascending |D*|) first so 46-word segments choose while the level is near anchors-only (their anchors-only bad mass max ~0.72) and large-|D| segments absorb received damage. Left-to-right greedy is not load-bearing.

R6 (L4 restated as convergence, not invariance): (i) local configuration law mu_k(d) converges geometrically to a fixed point mu_inf(d) — mechanism: exact recursion (anchors in the M-image of a parent ball ARE the parent configuration), bounded refill (excursion <= 8) with mean-reverting refill density (corr -0.3..-0.4), and fold contamination decaying x~0.35/level since M triples remote distances while stitching adds <= 8; (ii) far-field: uniform per-3-shell decay ratio r < 1 (measured 0.34-0.61) PLUS one-new-shell-per-level accumulation, so ledger constants creep monotonically toward a finite geometric limit (predicted at-birth word-kill limit ~0.52-0.55); the lemma must bound the LIMIT below the L3' threshold and prove the +3.5-4%/level at-birth creep contracts (~x0.45/level) — currently unresolved, decisive at level 7.

R7 (subpopulations): the lemma must explicitly handle the level-stable fragile subpopulation (46-word steps = 11-14% of segments at all levels, length-5 escape used at ~1% of them, flat) with a worst-case availability argument (min observed: 4 words at birth, 1 vs completed level), and treat boundary/early-walk segments (systematically hotter) separately with the boundary fraction shown O(1).
synthesis.proof_sketch_or_counterexample:
SYNTHESIS OF THREE ATTACKS + INDEPENDENT ADJUDICATION. Verdicts reconcile cleanly: attacks 1 and 3 prove L3-as-stated FALSE (I independently confirmed both counterexample classes today); attack 2 shows L4-as-stated (exact invariance) is false but convergence is real. The chain survives only as the repaired pair below; PROVABLE_WITH_REPAIRS refers to that pair — the PROOF-SKELETON.md forms are dead.

(1) CURRENT BEST STATEMENTS.
L3' (per-segment existence for the invariant-constrained construction): Fix S (124 radius-2 steps) and M=((3,0,0),(0,0,-3),(0,3,-1)). Let D*(s)=legal words of length 2-5 for steps with |D_2-4(s)|<~2000, else length 2-4. The construction stitches segments in ascending |D*(s)| order and enforces per-(donor,receiver) damage caps c_{i,j}=min(3*e_bar(shell(i,j)), c_max) with receiver budget sum_i c_{i,j}=beta~0.25. CLAIM: at every state reachable by THIS construction, every unstitched segment i satisfies, in exact word-union mass on D*(s_i): P_i(own-line BAD) + received-so-far + sum_{j unstitched} e(i,j)/c_{i,j} + triple-newborn <= 1-delta (target delta>=0.15), hence a stitch word exists. All constants in word-union mass (honest values: unrepaired greedy mean ~0.56, p99 ~0.91, max 1.0 — the order+caps must pull the pointwise max under 1-delta; the length-5 layer un-jams the three certified counterexamples with live mass 0.066-0.194 — thin).
L4' (ledger convergence): (i) the local configuration law within distance d of an interior segment converges geometrically in level to a fixed point (contraction measured x0.074-0.22/level; mechanism = exact M-recursion + bounded mean-reverting refill + geometric fold recession); (ii) fatal word-mass shell decomposition decays at a level-uniform per-3-shell ratio <1 (measured 0.34-0.61) while support grows one shell per level, so every L3' constant increases monotonically to a finite limit (predicted at-birth word-kill limit 0.52-0.55); L4' asserts the limits keep the L3' ledger below 1-delta, including the worst-case fragile-step segments, with boundary segments O(1). The unresolved threat is the measured +3.5-4%/level at-birth creep whose contraction is unproven at current precision.
L1, L2, L5 stand as before, except L5's induction hypothesis must carry the L3' invariant (caps + order), which is what excludes probe777-type states.

(2) VIABILITY. Structurally viable but demoted: the '22% headroom by counting alone' never existed (units error, independently confirmed: position mass 0.246 vs word-kill 0.565 E1-only on the same segments, ratio 2.0-2.6x). What genuinely survives and is strong: L1/L2 proven; exact recursion machine-verified across 6 levels; damage locality in space (7-30x decay per 3-shell) and time (60% of received mass from <=3 segments back); corrected constants level-stable/convergent across 7 seeds; an identified contraction mechanism for L4'. What is thin: worst-segment margins 0.07-0.19 on the extended space, and the monotone creep. The decisive gate is a single computation (below); if it passes, the remaining mathematics is hard but well-posed (prove the fixed-point contraction from the recursion — plausible; prove far-field incidence-thinning decay — the hardest piece, currently only dimension-counting heuristics; worst-case availability at fragile steps).

(3) HONEST ODDS (blunt). The pre-attack posture ('full proof skeleton, ~0.78<1, remaining: scale-invariance and review') was substantially an illusion created by a wrong-units constant and an over-broad quantifier; the certified walk itself violated the stated L3 three times. Post-repair: P(the repaired construction passes the level-5/6/7 pointwise audit) ~ 40-50% — the margins are real but thin and one measured trend points the wrong way. P(full referee-proof theorem | audit passes) ~ 25-30% — the far-field bound and worst-case simultaneity at ~0.1 margins are genuinely hard. Net: ~10-15% that this chain becomes a complete proof, most likely failure modes being (a) the at-birth creep failing to contract at level 7, (b) the far-field decay resisting proof. That is down from the implicit near-certainty of the ledger note, but up from zero-with-extra-steps: this is now an honest, falsifiable research programme with one experiment that decides whether to continue.
synthesis.computations_run: Independent adjudication of the two load-bearing disputed facts, /tmp/adjudicate_l3.py (PyPy, exact integer arithmetic, data from /Users/erik/homelab/math193: amplified-193-8292.txt, connector_domains4.pkl, collar_multiplicity4.json). (1) Reconstructed the certified 8293-point walk and segmented it by greedy M-preimage anchor matching (2477 anchors matched incl. ~19 coincidental preimages vs provenance's 2458; key facts robust to this): stitch-length histogram {2:91, 3:1433, 4:949, 5:3} — exactly 3 length-5 stitches, at corner steps (2,2,-2),(2,2,-2),(-2,-2,2), each with |D_2-4|=46, corroborating attack 1's segments 546/1074/1429. (2) Exact survivor check at those three segments (all 46 words, obstacles = placed prefix + future anchors, E1 + intra-word E2 + repeats): 0 surviving legal 2-4-step words at each — L3-as-stated's counterexample confirmed with independent code. (3) Units audit on 24 random segments at decision time: multiplicity-weighted fatal POSITION mass mean 0.246 / max 0.362 vs exact E1-only fatal WORD fraction mean 0.565 / max 0.734, per-segment ratio 2.03-2.60 — confirms the published 0.317-style constant is in the wrong units and the 0.78<1 budget does not close (E1-only mean + 1/3 Markov tax = 0.90 mean, 1.07 max, before E2/cross terms). Also read and cross-checked PROOF-SKELETON.md, REPORT.md, and logs/{shell-decomp,conditional-cross,triple-newborn,fatal-mass,fatal-trend,shell-levels,live-constants}.log against all three attack reports.
synthesis.decisive_next_computation: One computation gates everything: implement the repaired construction (length-5 connector layers for all steps with |D_2-4| < ~2000 via meet-in-the-middle; fragile-first ascending-|D*| stitch order; per-receiver caps c_{i,j} = min(3*e_bar(shell(i,j)), c_max) with beta ~ 0.25) and run it end-to-end from the certified seed-193 base through levels 5, 6, and 7 (~96k steps at L7; overnight PyPy), recording at EVERY stitch the exact word-union ledger P_i(BAD_i) + sum_{j unstitched} e(i,j)/c_{i,j}, then verify each completed level triple-free. Pre-registered pass criteria: (a) max ledger over all segments <= ~0.85 at every level with no upward drift L5->L6->L7; (b) at-birth word-kill mean increments contract ~x0.45/level toward the predicted 0.52-0.55 limit (a third linear +4% increment at L7 kills L4'); (c) fixed-point checks: N81 = 112.6 +/- 0.7, KS(L6,L7) below critical at d<=81, shell decay ratios 0.42-0.57 including the newly populated shell, fragile-step jam rate ~1% flat, P(fold<=81) ~ 1.4%. Pass -> the repaired chain is empirically closed and effort shifts to proving the two hard lemmas (local contraction from the exact recursion; far-field per-shell decay bound). Fail on (a) or (b) -> the chain is dead as constituted and the honest move is to redesign the menu/matrix for fatter margins rather than patch the ledger again.
